title: delphinium parishii subsp. subglobosum
intro: Delphinium parishii subsp. subglobosum, also known as the globular parish larkspur, is a perennial plant species in the delphinium genus, characterized by its globe-shaped flowers and slender stems. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Delphinium parishii subsp. subglobosum include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and soothe respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of the plant are often made into teas, tinctures, or salves, although it is essential to note that the plant's safety and efficacy have not been extensively scientifically studied.
